observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate adjustment of valid
reconciling items in the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of the
account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements at any
given time;
b. Municipal Treasurer to cause the timely submission to the Municipal
Accounting Office of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting
documents and the details of collections received from taxpayers directly
deposited in the depository bank of the Municipality for proper recording in
the books of accounts;
c. Municipal Treasurer to install procedures to be followed by the taxpayers
opted to pay their taxes directly to the depository bank of the Municipality
which will be posted in the conspicuous places for proper information of the
taxpayers; and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 on the preparation of the bank
reconciliation statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw the journal entry to adjust the Cash -
Local Treasury account with the net variance of P357,733.36 duly supported
by certified reconciliation statements of accounting and treasury’s records;
and
b. Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation
of the Cash-Local Treasury in all funds at least quarterly as directed under
Section 181 (c) of COA Circular No. 91-368 dated December 19, 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the
entire Municipality’s PPE and correspondingly prepare and submit to the
Audit Team the required inventory report thereon;
b. direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO, to conduct the
required reconciliation of their records with the inventory report and adjust
their records, if necessary, to present the true balance of PPE accounts in
the financial Statements;
c. direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that PPELCs are properly and
completely maintained for all PPE accounts; and henceforth, strictly adhere
to the provisions of the above-mentioned regulations to come up with a
reliable PPE balance.

We strongly reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due
to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected
accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
henceforth,
b. conduct periodic reconciliation of the account balances of Due from LGUs and Due
to LGUs in the Municipality books against their reciprocal accounts reflected in the
books of the Province to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two
records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant,
Human Resource and Management Officer and Municipal Treasurer to: (a) strictly
deduct from the salaries of the concerned employees their respective GSIS loan
and subsequently remit in full the loan amortizations to the GSIS; and
(b) henceforth, strictly comply with the cited regulation on the remittance of GSIS
loan to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniencies to the
concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC thru the BAC
Secretariat to strictly comply with the posting requirements, as well as
submission of the required reports in electronic format to the GPPB within the
period prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution
No. 11-2020, and its related Circular No. 02-2020.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. the Inventory Committee to submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE not later than
January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I;
b. the CGSO to maintain complete property cards for each type of property to
record the receipt and disposition of the property; and
c. the CGSO and City Accounting Office to reconcile the property cards with the
property ledger balances to determine the discrepancies noted and prepare the
necessary corrections/adjustments, if necessary.

We recommended that the General Services Officer to:
a. secure copies of the OR/CR of the motorcycles and motor vehicles listed in
Appendices F and G in order to check the validity of their registration and
thereafter facilitate the renewal of registration of units with expired registration,
if any; and
b. initiate the renewal of registration with the LTO of all serviceable motor vehicles
in compliance with Section 1 of Batas Pambansa Bilang 74, to avoid payments
of penalties for late registration and for damages to third party liabilities in case
of accidents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and the City
Assessor to:
a. send Notice of Delinquency to delinquent real property owners; and
b. undertake legal remedies and a more aggressive tax collection strategies
provided under RA No. 7160 to improve real property tax collection efficiency.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to review all the
accounting entries that affect the aforementioned intra-agency accounts to
determine the cause(s) of the discrepancies noted between the reported balances
and thereafter make the necessary adjustments to reconcile the said accounts to
ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local School Board to:
a. stop charging expenses not allowable against the SEF; and
b. utilize the fund only for the allowable or priority expenses provided under
Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 4.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s.
2017.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. the City Accountant to:
a.1 maintain subsidiary ledgers for the unexpended LDRRMF transferred to the
Trust Fund books for proper and accurate monitoring of the fund; and
a.2 include the details of the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in the disclosure of
the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements in compliance with
Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
b. CDRRMC to include in the City’s LDRRMFIP, under a separate caption, the
list of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of the reported transactions of the Cash - Local Treasury and CIB - LCCA accounts in the accounting ledgers and Treasury’s cashbooks in order to determine the causes of the discrepancies; and
b. Municipal Accountant to immediately effect the necessary adjustments to avoid the accumulation of the discrepancy, as well as to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Conducted regular reconciliation of municipal records and continuously identified discrepancies. No Management Action taken. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 31, Part II of this report. The finding first raised: CY 2010 (10+years).
observations.reason: Non-recording of adjustment found in reconciliation. Year when the finding first raised: CY 2010 (10+years).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to come up with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of taxpayers and the amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the rules on the setting-up of the RPT and SET Receivables to ensure the fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury submitted a certified list of taxpayers and the amounts collectible for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Legal Officer, to exhaust all possible means to collect the said balances, and the Municipal Legal Officer to exhaust all possible legal means to collect the dormant accounts; and
b. prepare all the necessary supporting documents for the request for write-off.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal Legal Office for the exhaust of all possible legal means to collect the dormant accounts. Preparation of all the necessary supporting documents for the refiling of the request for write-off is still on-going.
observations.reason: In-process

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant disclosed that communications were continuously done to reconcile the accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No. 38, Part II of this report. Year when the finding first raised: CY 2019 (2 years).

observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO explained that they conducted the required complete physical count of all the municipal properties. She noted that some of the PPE on the list of not included in the count was due to the failure of Engineering Office to provide the reports needed to start the physical inventory. Also, the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensures that efforts were exerted to complete the records of the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: PPELCs and Property Cards were still incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule for Road Networks and make the necessary adjustments to correct the records and balances of the Road Network Systems and its related accounts; and
b. GSO to maintain and/or prepare the required records and reports for the Road Network in the System in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Maintained and prepared the required records and prepared the Lapsing Schedule for Road Networks. GSO prepared the required records for the Road Network system but report on physical Road Networks was not submitted.
observations.reason: Non-submission of Report on Physical Road Networks.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to review/analyze all the existing fund transfers in the books and exert more effort on the retrieval of the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) and/or Trust Agreements relative therewith to properly utilize the available funds; and
b. Implementing Office/Division and/or personnel assigned to fast track the implementation and accomplishment of the projects so that the benefits would be delivered to the intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible time. Otherwise, the unutilized funds must be returned to the source NGAs in order that it may be made available for other priority programs or projects of said government agencies.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and analyzed the fund transfers for the implementation of PPAs of these fund transfers. Implementing offices returned unutilized fund transfers to the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to strictly abide with the guidelines governing the proper recording and recognition in the books of accounts, the funds received from the National Government;
b. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents; and
c. Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the posting requirements of GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 for information purposes and transparency.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking supporting documents. Bids and Awards Committee complied with posting requirements of GPPB.
The Municipal offices submitted some supporting documents; some liquidation and communications with DSWD were ongoing. On-going actions for liquidation; some justifications were submitted.
observations.reason: On-going. Presence of unliquidated cash advances related to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the GAD Focal Point System continue to lead the Municipality in its immediate implementation of gender-responsive PPAs and ensure that gender-responsive PPAs were included therein, which are aligned with measures to mitigate the effects of COVID-19 and the new normal to promote effective survivor-centered response.
observations.managementAction: Conducted the immediate implementation of GAD PPAs aligned with COVID-19 response. Prepared Certificate of Review and Endorsement of GPB CY 2021 issued by DILG on October 29, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to lead the Municipality in its immediate implementation of PPAs to conduct COVID-19 response related PPAs to mitigate the risks caused by the pandemic.
observations.managementAction: Conducted and implemented immediate PPAs to mitigate the risks caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials to reconcile their records and reports as to the utilization of the 20% DF and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification. Also, ensure that the quarterly reports on the utilization of the 20% DF are submitted to DOF-BLGF as prescribed by the JMC.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted the reports. Municipal offices reconciled records and submitted quarterly utilization reports to COA and DOF-BLGF.
observations.reason: Delay in submission of required reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the LDRRMO to strictly follow the guidelines in the preparation of LDRRMFIP and to submit to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), the Department of the Interior and Local Government-Municipal Local Government Operations Office (DILG-MLGOO) and to the COA, a copy of fully compliant LDRRMFIP; and
b. require the LDRRMO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted the LDRRMFIP to OCD, DILG and COA. Submitted some monthly utilization reports to OCD via email; however, submission to DILG-MLGOO not properly observed for all months.
observations.reason: Delay in submission of monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. Year when the finding first raised: CY 2013 (9 years).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWMB convene regularly to ensure that the review and updating of the Solid Waste Management Plan will be conducted on time or as the need arises.
observations.managementAction: MSWMB conducted dry-run of WACS in Barangay Mahabang Parang targeted to complete the study and updating by September 2022.
observations.reason: Subject to the completion of the study and updating of the 10-year plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. derecognize in the books of accounts the unreleased loans from LBP totaling P734,314,593.50 by debiting the Loans Payable – Domestic account and crediting Loans Receivable – GOCCs accounts both by the same amount; and
b. recognize the liability from borrowings only upon receipt of the advice from the bank informing the release of the proceeds, in compliance with Chapter 3, Section 25 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accountant had drawn Journal Entry Voucher No. 101-2021-06-J0254 dated June 23, 2021 to reverse the original entry made.
In CY 2021, five loans totaling P491,925,871.36 were recorded in the books only upon receipt of the advice from the bank informing the release of the proceeds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. derecognize the unperformed/ unbilled portion of ongoing construction projects totaling P76,314,593.20 on the books of account by debiting the AP account and crediting CIP account both by the same amount; and
b. recognize liability only when the goods and services are accepted or rendered to the supplier/creditor when the goods and bills are received or delivered and services are rendered.
We also recommended:
c. revert the six dormant AP totaling P222,524.28 proven to be without valid claims to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and
d. coordinate with the concerned payees of the Accounts Payable totaling P26,848,078.00 for the immediate completion of required lacking documents to be able to proceed with the settlement of obligations; and
e. ensure that all past due and valid unpaid obligations are covered by perfected contracts duly supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accountant had drawn Journal Entry Voucher No. 101-2021-07-J0322 dated July 30, 2021 to reverse the original entry made.
In CY 2021, the City recognize liability only when the goods and services are received or bills are received or delivered and services are rendered.
In CY 2021, the City Accountant had drawn Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 221-2021-03-J0036 dated March 31, 2021 and 221-2021-10-J0139 dated October 25, 2021 to record the adjustment.
AP and portion of the total Accounts Payable, while P25,000,000.00 were recorded in Accounts Payable, the remaining P1,848,078.00 will be subjected to final documentary confirmation.
All past due and valid unpaid obligations are covered by perfected contracts and with complete documentation.
observations.reason: The City is still waiting to resolve the dispute on the subject land related to the P26,848,078.00 aged AP; moreover, AP and portion of the total Accounts Payable aged four to six years were still not settled as of date due to issues related to documentary confirmation. Some coordination with concerned payees has been undertaken; P25,000,000.00 was recorded, while the remaining P1,848,078.00 will be subjected to final documentary confirmation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
• City Accountant and City General Services Officer to:
a. investigate/determine the causes of discrepancies in prior years between their respective records and make necessary adjustments to ensure accuracy, reliability and existence of PPEs and to:
    a.1 book up the unrecorded 89 PPE items with the accumulated cost of PPEs amounting to P24,622,626.00 and the unrecognized depreciation;
    a.2 reclassify the improperly recorded PPE under Intangible Asset; and
    a.3 review records of PPEs recorded in lump-sum of P16,680,191.05 and identify the item/kind/category with a complete description, property number and location for easy identification/comparison of each PPE item with the property records.
• City General Services Officer to:
    b. ensure the existence of 79 PPE items totaling P85,058,655.31 recorded in the books but not found at the station/counted during inventory, otherwise, identify the person/s accountable/liable and return the value of the PPE at the time of loss; including these items in the RPCPPE;
    c. exclude in the RPCPPE semi-expendable property totaling P9,781,080.55 and ensure that Inventory Custodian Slip for monitoring and control purposes are prepared upon issuance; and
    d. reclassify the PPE items totaling P14,237,700.00 in the RPCPPE in the same account used by the accounting records as prescribed under Chapter 2, Section 57 and Annex 49 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II to facilitate reconciliation.
observations.managementAction:
As of October 6, 2021, reconciliation of respective records and the RPCPPE with a net difference of P155,313.00 and make the necessary adjustments accordingly. The City General Services Office cited several causes of the remaining discrepancies such as timing of recording and different basis of classification of significant PPEs, among others.
The City General Services Office made the necessary adjustments in its records, while the City Accounting Office draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the improperly recorded PPE.
The subject semi-expendable properties were excluded in the RPCPPE and Inventory Custodian Slips were prepared upon issuance. The City General Services Office was able to locate PPE items and subsequently included in the DVs and RPCPPE. The City General Services Office is still trying to locate the remaining PPEs and its relevant purchase orders in order to have a basis before including these items in the RPCPPE.
The City Accountant had drawn Journal Entry Voucher No. 101-2021-04-J0155 dated April 1, 2021 to reclassify the improperly recorded PPE. The City Accounting Office was able to sufficiently describe and properly identify the noted PPEs recorded in lump-sum.
observations.reason: The City General Services Office is still trying to locate the remaining PPEs and its relevant purchase orders in order to have a basis before including these items in the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer, to:
a. cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted DVs to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension, which may mature into disallowance; and
b. observe timeliness in the submission of paid DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the end of the month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The City officials were able to submit the 56 unsubmitted DVs. The remaining six (6) unsubmitted DVs were still being located.
observations.reason: Not strict compliance to the prescribed period of submission of all paid DVs was not yet feasible due to the several steps needed to be done before the submission of the accounts to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. provide Allowance for Impairment for the noted dormant receivable accounts for the possible losses; and
b. conduct necessary procedures to secure/produce all relevant and applicable documents enumerated under Item 8.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to support the filing of request for authority to write-off of noted dormant receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accountant has provided an Allowance for Impairment for the noted dormant receivable accounts.
The City Mayor has submitted a request for authority to the Commission on Audit to write-off the dormant receivable accounts, authority supported with some certifications.
observations.reason: Management is still completing the required documents to support the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reclassify the noted donations from Trust Liability – DRRMF to Government Equity account.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, to:
a. determine the other causes of the discrepancy of P3,273,174.22 in the reported unutilized balance of LDRRMF between the books and Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF and make the necessary adjustment; and
b. prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accountant had drawn Journal Entry Voucher No. 101-2021-07-J0323 dated July 31, 2021 to take up the adjustment.
The City Accountant explained that the noted discrepancy was primarily due to the unutilized balance of LDRRMF aside from those mentioned above and made the necessary adjustment.
The City Accounting Office was able to submit the monthly report within the prescribed period for some months.
observations.reason: The City was able to submit the monthly reports for some months (May and June 2021) but not strictly within the prescribed period for all months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the City as against their corresponding accounts in the books of the Province and effect the necessary adjustments accordingly in the books.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant sent a confirmation letter dated August 24, 2021 to the Provincial Accountant, wherein the latter confirmed that the recorded amounts in the books of the City are the same with that recorded in the books of the Province.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the concerned Requesting Unit to submit the required lacking documents such as Omnibus Sworn Statement, Inspection and Acceptance Report, and photos of items purchased and the distribution thereof; and ensure that all claims are paid based on duly processed DVs with complete supporting documents, in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
b. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to immediately post the noted 16 procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement to the GPPB Online Portal, in compliance with the pertinent provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and GPPB-TSO Advisory No. 05-2020.
c. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Information Officer to update the City’s website and make the necessary posting of the monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the BGCM as part of their full disclosure policy and send written notice to DBM, House of Representatives, Senate, House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance that the said reports have already been posted on the City’s website.
d. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare the quarterly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the LGSF – COVID-19 Financial Assistance to LGUs and furnish the same to the City Information Officer for posting in the City’s website; and the City Information Officer to post on the City’s website the quarterly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the LGSF – COVID-19 Financial Assistance to LGUs as part of their full disclosure policy once the Local Finance Committee has already furnished the said report. Also, send written notice to DBM, Speaker, Senate and relevant committees upon posting.
observations.managementAction:
a. The City officials were able to submit the complete lacking documents enumerated such as Omnibus Sworn Statement, Inspection and Acceptance Report, and photos of items purchased and the distribution thereof. Disbursements pertaining to emergency procurements are supported with complete documentation.
b. The City’s BAC Secretariat was able to submit proof of posting in the GPPB Online Portal of the 16 projects.
c. The City’s Information Officer provided printouts/screenshots of the electronic mails sent to the required offices informing them that the required reports have been posted in the City’s official website. The City Information Officer also submitted screenshots of the report posted in the City’s official website and facebook page.
d. The Local Finance Committee prepared the required quarterly report and furnished the City Information Officer for posting. The City Information Officer submitted screenshots of the report posted in the City’s official website.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Assessor, in coordination with the City Treasurer, to:
a. ensure that the 6,867 real property recorded under the name of government entities were actually registered under those names and that the 56 real property under the name of schools and churches are actually, directly, and exclusively used for religious or educational purposes:
    ▪ if so, exclude those real property in the assessment roll; or
    ▪ if the real property recorded under the name of government entities were proven to be already transferred to a private individual, update the assessment roll with the name of the registered owner of the property and enforce collection of delinquent RPT; or
    ▪ if the real property recorded under the name of schools and churches were proven to be not directly, and exclusively used for religious, or educational purposes, enforce collection of delinquent RPT.
b. verify the registered owners of the 573 real property recorded under the name of the noted homeowner’s associations and see to it that they are duly registered with the appropriate government agency to acquire legal personality and to allow the City to enforce collection of delinquent RPT;
c. exclude from the assessment roll the noted 14 real property registered under the name of PEZA registered ECOZONE enterprises which are already availing the five percent GIT incentive; and
d. exert efforts to identify other real property tagged as “discovery”, aside from the noted 29 residential buildings, conduct ocular inspection and re-assessment thereof and issue a notice of assessment to be able to update the assessment roll and enforce payment of delinquent RPT.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor’s Office, in coordination with the City Treasurer’s Office, was able to identify that some of the subject real properties were actually owned by the National Government and specific homeowners association properties were identified. The City Assessor’s Office was able to identify that some of the said real properties were actually owned by specific homeowners associations and some properties were actually owned by the National Government.
observations.reason: Still subject to evaluation as to the ownership of the property.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to:
a. settle the right of way issues immediately for the contractor to hasten project implementation; 
b. henceforth, ensure that detailed engineering activities are sufficiently carried out before awarding the contract and/or implementing the projects to identify and immediately address possible problems, in accordance with Annex A to the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184; and
c. require the contractor to cause the extension of the validity of the performance security to cover all the approved contract time extensions until issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the City as an assurance of the faithful performance and compliance of obligations by the contractor.
observations.managementAction: The City was able to settle the right of way issues of five affected properties. Detailed engineering activities were carried out for the infrastructure projects in CY 2021. The contractor was able to extend the validity of the performance security to cover the approved contract time extensions until issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance.
observations.reason: Undergoing settlement of right of way issues of five (5) affected properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. strictly require the AOs to liquidate their CAs within the prescribed period and supported with complete documentary requirements under COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004; and
b. refrain from granting additional CA to any official, officer or employee without liquidating first his/her prior unliquidated CA, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: CAs granted to AOs were liquidated within the prescribed period and supported with complete documentation. Additional CAs were granted only to AOs with no prior unliquidated CAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. submit justification on the use of reimbursement as a mode of payment for the noted disbursements and submit the required lacking documents enumerated under Item 4 of the same Circular; 
b. discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services through reimbursement basis, except for petty expenses to be paid out of the Petty Cash Fund; and
c. strictly follow the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 on the use of Alternative Methods of Procurement, if competitive bidding is not feasible.
observations.managementAction: The City submitted justification on the use of reimbursement as a mode of payment for the noted disbursements and submitted the required lacking documents. Disbursements were supported with complete documentation. The City discontinued the practice and strictly follows the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 on the use of Alternative Methods of Procurement, where applicable.

observations.recommendation:

a. derecognize in the books of accounts the unreleased loans from LBP totaling
P734,314,593.50 by debiting the Loans Payable – Domestic account and
crediting Loans Receivable – GOCCs accounts both by the same amount; and

b. recognize the liability from borrowings only upon receipt of the advice from
the bank or lending agency informing the release of the proceeds, in
compliance with Chapter 3, Section 25 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

account and crediting CIP account both by the same amount; and

b. recognize liability only when the goods and services are accepted or

b. coordinate with the concerned payees of the two Accounts Payable totaling
P26,848,078.00 for the immediate completion of required lacking
documents to proceed with the settlement of obligations; and

c. ensure that all past due and valid unpaid obligations are covered by
perfected contracts duly supported with complete documentation.

reliability and existence of PPE account balances as reflected in the financial
statements; and

per book and per RPCPPE with a net difference of P155,313.00 and make the
necessary adjustments accordingly.

City Accountant to:

P24,622,626.00 and the unrecognized depreciation;

b. reclassify the improperly recorded PPE under Intangible Asset; and

c. review records of PPEs recorded in lump-sum of P16,680,191.05 and identify
the item/kind/category with a complete description, property number and
location for easy identification/comparison of each PPE item with the property
records.

City General Services Officer to:

a. ensure the existence of 79 PPE items totaling P85,058,655.31 recorded in the
books but not found at the station/counted during inventory, otherwise,
identify the person/s accountable/liable therefore to return the value of the PPE
at the time of loss;

b. exclude in the RPCPPE semi-expendable property totaling P9,781,080.55 and
ensure that Inventory Custodian Slip covered all these upon issuance for
monitoring and control purposes; and

c. reclassify the PPE items totaling P14,237,700.00 in the RPCPPE in the same
account used by the accounting records as prescribed under Chapter 2,
Section 57 and Annex 49 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II to facilitate
reconciliation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, in coordination
with the City Treasurer, to:

a. cause the immediate submission of the 62 unsubmitted DVs to avoid issuance of
Notice of Suspension, which may mature into disallowance; and

b. observe timeliness in the submission of paid DVs and their supporting
documents to the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the end of the
month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. provide Allowance for Impairment for the noted dormant receivable accounts
for the possible losses; and

b. conduct necessary procedures to secure/produce all relevant and applicable
documents enumerated under Item 8.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to
support the filing of request for authority to write-off of noted dormant
receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to make the
necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reclassify the noted
donations from Trust Liability – DRRMF to the Government Equity account.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, in
coordination with the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer,
to:

a. determine the other causes of the discrepancy of P3,273,174.22 in the
reported unutilized balance of LDRRMF between the books and Report on
Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF aside from those mentioned above and
make the necessary adjustment; and

b. prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office
of the Auditor.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to coordinate
with the Provincial Accountant to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of Due
from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the City as against their corresponding
accounts in the books of the Province and effect the necessary adjustments
accordingly in the books.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

• coordinate with the concerned Requesting Unit to submit the required lacking
documents complete or supplied with relevant information on the transactions
listed in Appendix J; and

• ensure that all claims are paid based on duly processed DVs with complete
supporting documents, in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Secretariat to immediately post the noted 16 procurement projects
undertaken through Emergency Procurement to the GPPB Online Portal, in
compliance with the pertinent provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and
GPPB-TSO Advisory No. 05-2020.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Information Officer to
update the City’s website and make the necessary posting of the monthly
Report of Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the BGCM as
part of their full disclosure policy.

We also recommended that the City Mayor send written notice to the DBM,
House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on
Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance and other required offices
informing that the said reports have already been posted on the City’s website.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
• Local Finance Committee to prepare the quarterly Report of Fund
Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the LGSF – COVID-19
Financial Assistance to LGUs and furnish the same to the City Information
Officer for posting in the City’s website; and
• City Information Officer to post on the City’s website the quarterly Report
of Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the LGSF – COVID-
19 Financial Assistance to LGUs as part of their full disclosure policy once
the Local Finance Committee has already furnished the said report.

We also recommended that the City Mayor send written notice to the
Secretary of DBM, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the
Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on
Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance upon posting of the reports
on the City’s website.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Assessor, in coordination
with the City Treasurer, to:

a. ensure that the 6,867 real property recorded under the noted government
entities were actually registered under those names, and that the 56 real
property under the name of schools and churches are actually, directly, and
exclusively used for religious or educational purposes:
▪ if so, exclude those real property in the assessment roll; or
▪ if the real property recorded under the name of government entities were
proven to be already transferred to a private individual, update the
assessment roll with the name of the registered owner of the property and
enforce collection of delinquent RPT; and
▪ if the real property recorded under the name of schools and churches were
proven to be not directly, and exclusively used for religious, or educational
purposes, enforce collection of delinquent RPT.

b. verify the registered owners of the 573 real property recorded under the name
of the noted homeowner’s associations and see to it that they are duly
registered with the appropriate government agency to acquire legal personality
and to allow the City to enforce collection of delinquent RPT;

c. exclude from the assessment roll the noted 14 real property registered under
the name of PEZA-registered ECOZONE enterprises which are already availing
the five per cent GIT incentive; and

d. exert efforts to identify other real property tagged as “discovery”, aside from
the noted 29 residential buildings, conduct ocular inspection and re-
assessment thereof and issue a notice of assessment to be able to update the
assessment roll and enforce payment of delinquent RPT.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to:

a. settle the right of way issues and other problems immediately for the
contractor to hasten project implementation; and

b. henceforth, ensure that detailed engineering activities are sufficiently
carried out before awarding the contract and/or implementing the projects
to identify and immediately address possible problems, in accordance with
Annex A of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to require the
contractor to cause the extension of the validity of the performance security to
cover all the approved contract time extensions until issuance of the Certificate
of Final Acceptance by the City as an assurance of the faithful performance and
compliance of obligations by the contractor.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. strictly require the AOs to liquidate their CAs supported with complete documentary
requirements within the period prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and
2012-004; and

b. refrain from granting additional CA to any official, officer or employee without
liquidating first his/her prior CA.

We recommended that Management:

a. submit justification on the use of reimbursement as a mode of payment for the
noted disbursements and the non-adherence to the GPPB Circular No. 01-
2020;

b. submit the required lacking documents enumerated under Item 4 of the same
Circular;

c. discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services through
reimbursement basis, except for petty expenses to be paid out of the Petty Cash
Fund; and

d. strictly follow the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 on the use of Alternative Methods
of Procurement, if competitive bidding is not feasible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continuously conduct
a thorough review and reconciliation of the reported transactions of the
Cash - Local Treasury and CIB - LCCA accounts in the accounting ledgers
and Treasury’s cashbooks in order to determine the causes of the
discrepancies; and
b. Municipal Accountant to immediately effect the necessary adjustments to
avoid the accumulation of the discrepancy, as well as to ensure the
correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to come
up with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of the
taxpayers and the amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a reliable
RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the
rules on the setting-up of the RPT and SET Receivables to ensure the fair
presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Legal Officer, to exhaust all possible means to
collect the retained dormant accounts; and
b. prepare all the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the
Municipality’s refiling of the request for write-off in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2016-05.

We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that
appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair
presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the entire
Municipality’s PPE and to conduct reconciliation of the results of the
inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancy
between the inventory report and the accounting and property records
should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error
was committed. Submit to the Auditor the reconciled RPCPPE, pursuant to
Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to ensure that their respective records,
PPELCs and PCs are being completely maintained. Both records should
always be updated and reconciled; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete lapsing schedule for all the
depreciable property of the Municipality and furnish the Audit Team for
verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule for Road Networks
and make the necessary adjustments to correct the records and balances of
the Road Network Systems and its related Accumulated Depreciation and
Depreciation Expense accounts; and
b. GSO to maintain and/or prepare Local Road Inventory and Road Map,
LRNPC and RLRN in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to review/analyze all the existing fund transfers in the books
and exert more effort on the retrieval of the Memorandum of Agreements
(MOAs) and/or Trust Agreements relative therewith to properly utilize the
available funds; and
b. Implementing Office/Division and/or personnel assigned to fast track the
implementation and accomplishment of the projects so that the benefits
would be delivered to the intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible
time. Otherwise, the unutilized funds must be returned to the source NGAs
in order that it may be made available for other priority programs or projects
of said government agencies.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
observations.managementAction: The current and non-current portions of long-term loans payable are now being classified accordingly in the Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Accountant and City General Services Officer to:
a. investigate/determine the causes of discrepancies in prior years between their respective records and make necessary adjustments to ensure the accuracy, reliability and existence of PPE account balances as reflected in the financial statements; and
b. undertake the immediate reconciliation of PPEs recorded in different amounts per book and per RPCPPE with a net difference of P155,313.00 and make the necessary adjustments accordingly.
City Accountant to:
a. book up the unrecorded 89 PPE items with the accumulated cost of P24,622,626.00 and the unrecognized depreciation; 
b. reclassify the improperly recorded PPE under Intangible Asset; and
c. review records of PPEs recorded in lump-sum of P16,680,191.05 and identify the item/kind/category with a complete description, property number and location for easy identification/comparison of each PPE item with the property records.
City General Services Officer to:
a. ensure the existence of 79 PPE items totaling P85,058,655.31 recorded in the books but not found at the station/counted during inventory, otherwise, identify the person/s accountable/liable therefore to return the value of the PPE at the time of loss; 
b. exclude in the RPCPPE semi-expendable property totaling P9,781,080.55 and ensure that Inventory Custodian Slip covered all these upon issuance for monitoring and control purposes; and
c. reclassify the PPE items totaling P14,237,700.00 in the RPCPPE in the same account used by the accounting records as prescribed under Chapter 2, Section 57 and Annex 49 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II to facilitate reconciliation.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant and the City General Services Officer both agreed to reconcile to bring the two records into agreement.
observations.reason: The net difference between the books and the RPCPPE significantly increased to P524,144,665.39 from last year’s difference of P267,787,267.83 or by 95.73 per cent. The CAO and CGSO explained and admitted that the increase of the variance between their records was attributed to their inability to reconcile records related to current, and CY 2018 and prior transactions due to the COVID-19 pandemic and its related restrictions. Only the CY 2019 PPE transactions, which were the subject of audit findings and recommendations of the last year’s Annual Audit Report, were partially reconciled by the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
observations.managementAction: Accrual method of accounting is now being observed in the recording of advance payments for RPT and SET.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and/or the City Treasurer to:
a. send notice to the concerned payees of the four unclaimed checks and submit thereafter the related DVs upon release of checks; and
b. exert extra efforts to locate the 17 missing DVs and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension which may mature into disallowance; and
c. produce the documents needed to support the five DVs with lacking documents and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension which may mature into disallowance; and
d. observe timeliness in the submission of paid DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the end of the month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the City Treasurer: as of March 15, 2021, 28 DVs were paid and already submitted to the City Accounting Office as of December 31, 2020; six were canceled and submitted to the City Accounting Office as of February 28, 2021; 13 DVs were paid as of December 31, 2020 and submitted to the City Accounting Office on the succeeding year; and 15 DVs were unclaimed and reported stale and submitted to the CAO on December 2, 2020. The City Accountant commented that they would coordinate with the City Treasurer to submit the noted accounts immediately.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will validate the foregoing actions taken by Management. It should be emphasized that non-submission of DVs and their supporting documents may result in the issuance of Notice of Suspension, which will eventually mature to Disallowance if the requirements are not complied by the City Accountant and the City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Treasurer to send written notice to the concerned payees of the existence of the subject checks; and b. City Accountant to prepare a JEV for the cancellation of subject stale checks by debiting Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and crediting Accounts Payable accounts both by P21,446.40.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer maintained the Schedule of Unreleased Checks and forwarded them to the City Accounting Office. The noted unreleased checks as of December 31, 2019 were already released to the concerned payees during the year. The subject stale checks were already cancelled in the books of accounts per JEV No. J053 dated July 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. assess the collectability of the dormant receivable accounts and exert extra effort in the collection of the same; 
b. set-up the appropriate allowance for impairment for possible losses; and
c. if these receivables have been proven to be uncollectible and worthless, derecognize the assets and the corresponding allowance for impairment from the books of accounts through write-off of dormant account guided by the provisions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 on Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-Off of Dormant Receivable Accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant agreed with the recommendations and committed to coordinate with the City Mayor on the processing of the request for authority to write-off of the noted dormant receivables.
observations.reason: The collectability of the receivables was already proven to be remote by the Management. The City decided to request for the write-off of the noted dormant receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Assessor and City Treasurer to: a. ensure that the recorded real properties under the then Municipality of Biñan and National Housing Authority were registered under those agencies; b. verify the registered owners of the real properties recorded under the name of the homeowner’s association; and c. exert efforts to cleanse and update their assessment records to reflect the correct uncollected RPT from the aforementioned taxpayers and impose collection thereto.
observations.managementAction: In coordination with the City Treasurer, the City Assessor committed to exert efforts in cleansing and updating their assessment records to reflect the correct uncollected RPT from the aforementioned taxpayers and impose collection thereto.
observations.reason: The City Assessor did not provide the Audit Team any reason for the non-implementation of this recommendation. The City enacted Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance No. 4-2020 dated June 15, 2020. The City Treasurer is still coordinating with the City Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and City General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; b. City Accountant to provide proper disclosure of the Road Network Systems in the Notes to Financial Statements and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards; and c. City General Services Officer to maintain Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer submitted a letter and referred the matter to the City Engineering Office. The City Accounting Office and City General Services Office were requested to maintain the records. The City General Services Office was still in the process of maintaining/updating its Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards.
observations.reason: Details of Road Network Systems were still not disclosed due to the above reason. The City General Services Office is still in the process of maintaining/updating the Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to: a. revert the five APs without valid claims totaling P100,573.06 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445; b. coordinate with the concerned payees of the two APs totaling P26,848,078.00 for the immediate completion of required lacking documents to proceed with the settlement of obligations; and c. ensure that all past due and valid unpaid obligations are covered by perfected contracts duly supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant agreed with the recommendations and committed to revert the noted long outstanding APs. Continuous coordination and follow-up with the noted two suppliers for the completion of required documents to proceed with the settlement of the claims is ongoing.
observations.reason: The noted five APs without valid claims totaling P100,573.06 were to be reverted to the unappropriated surplus during the year but committed to revert the same in CY 2021. The concerned payees are still in the process of completing the required lacking documents to support the payments of their claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to: a. determine the cause of the difference of the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the errors; and b. analyze the identified discrepancies and errors in the recording of RPT and SET collections at the end of each month and make necessary adjustments. We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to: a. draw the necessary adjustments to reconcile the books to the balances of the reciprocal accounts; and b. ensure the correctness of JEVs being drawn before posting the same to avoid errors in the recording of transactions between reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: JEVs are now being double-checked for correctness before posting to General Ledger. Necessary adjustments in the books were already made per JEV Nos. J074 and J075 both dated February 9, 2020 and JEV No. J078 dated March 30, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the City as against their corresponding accounts in the books of the Province and effect the necessary adjustments accordingly in the books.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant agreed with the recommendations and committed to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation between their records.
observations.reason: The balances in the books of the City were still not reconciled with the corresponding balances in the books of the Province due to lack of coordination as of reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to analyze current and prior years transactions in all funds affecting the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of the said accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended that the City Accountant be required to conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the difference.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant reconciled the reciprocal accounts as of December 31, 2020 and adjustments were made accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Assessor to ensure that the recorded real properties under the noted agencies are correctly registered and the City Treasurer to intensify a tax campaign to maximize collections and avail of amnesty remedies provided under Sections 256 and 258 of RA No. 7160 to enforce collections.
observations.managementAction: In coordination with the City Treasurer, the City Assessor committed to exert efforts in cleansing and updating their assessment records to reflect the correct uncollected RPT from the aforementioned taxpayers and impose collection thereto.
observations.reason: The City Assessor did not provide the Audit Team any reason for the non-implementation of some measures. The City enacted SBO No. 4-2020 dated June 15, 2020; however, further reconciliation and updating of records is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Social Welfare and Development Officer to expedite the implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Program in accordance with the covering Specific Implementation Agreement, in order for the beneficiaries to enjoy the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Of the total amount of fund transfer, P1,806,084.00 was already implemented by the City. The remaining amount was to be reverted back to the DSWD because the purpose of the fund transfer could not be attained anymore due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: The remaining balance of P3,154,347.00 is to be reverted to the DSWD because the purpose of the fund transfer could not be attained due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to: a. impose the liquidated damages to the suppliers concerned and require the suppliers concerned to refund the same to the City; and b. ensure that all procurements are supported with Inspection and Acceptance Reports and/or POs with complete information in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineering Office submitted a letter informing that the liquidated damages were imposed and deducted from the payments to the suppliers. The noted Inspection and Acceptance Reports were submitted to the Office of the Auditor. The City Accountant will ensure that POs are filled up with all necessary information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC of the E-Trike Program to: a. enter into MOA with every e-trike driver/operator to establish drivers/operators responsibilities of both parties and terms and conditions with regards to the transfer of ownership to the drivers/operators; and b. strictly implement the terms and conditions of the MOA, including the collection of the leasing fee (boundary) on a daily basis as gradual payment for the e-trikes, including its proper remittance to the City Treasurer’s Office.
observations.managementAction: The OIC of the E-Trike Program recalled the 40 e-trike units and is in the process of re-screening qualified drivers/operators. They committed to enter into MOAs with drivers/operators who will be selected. Some of the e-trikes are currently under maintenance due to accidents or missing parts.
observations.reason: The OIC recalled the 40 e-trike units for re-screening of drivers/operators and MOAs are pending. Some e-trikes are under maintenance due to damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF details were disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements. However, the balance of Trust Liability – DRRMF account required further reconciliation.
observations.reason: The balances of Trust Liability – DRRMF account were not fully reconciled with the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the entire Municipality’s PPE and to conduct reconciliation of the results of the inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancy between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed. Submit to the Auditor the reconciled RPCPPE, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; b. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to ensure that their respective records, PPELCs and PCs are being completely maintained. Both records should always be updated and reconciled; and c. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete lapsing schedule for all the depreciable property of the Municipality and furnish the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: Conducted a partial inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and to conduct reconciliation of the results of the inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancy between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed. Submit to the Auditor the reconciled RPCPPE, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; Continued to maintain records but still not complete. Updating of Acknowledgment Receipts are on-going.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continuously conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of the reported transactions of the Cash - Local Treasury and CIB - LCCA accounts in the accounting ledgers and Treasury’s cashbooks in order to determine the causes of the discrepancies; and b. Municipal Accountant to immediately effect the necessary adjustments to avoid the accumulation of the discrepancy, as well as to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the OIC-Municipal Accountant, stated that efforts were being exerted to reconcile the accounting books with the Treasury’s records and assured their compliance with the Audit Team’s recommendations.
observations.reason: No action was taken by Management for the non implemenation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to come up with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of the taxpayers and the amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances; and b. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the rules on the setting-up of the RPT and SET Receivables to ensure the fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule for Road Networks and make the necessary adjustments to correct the records and balances of the Road Network Systems and its related Accumulated Depreciation and Depreciation Expense accounts; and b. GSO to maintain and/or prepare Local Road Inventory and Road Map, LRNPC and RLRN in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the Audit Team’s observation.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the non implemenation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. coordinate with the Municipal Legal Officer, to exhaust all possible means to collect the retained dormant accounts; and b. prepare all the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the Municipality’s refiling of the request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-05.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the audit deficiencies, and agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the OIC-Municipal Accountant, commented that they coordinated with the Rizal Provincial Government for the reconciliation of both of their records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to review/analyze all the existing fund transfers in the books and exert more effort on the retrieval of the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) and/or Trust Agreements relative therewith to properly utilize the available funds; and b. Implementing Office/Division and/or personnel assigned to fast track the implementation and accomplishment of the projects so that the benefits would be delivered to the intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible time. Otherwise, the unutilized funds must be returned to the source NGAs in order that it may be made available for other priority programs or projects of said government agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to strictly abide with the guidelines governing the proper recording and recognition in the books of accounts, the funds received from the National Government; b. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents; and c. Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the posting requirements of GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 for information purposes and transparency.
observations.managementAction: Management belatedly submitted the lacking documents to support the disbursements charged to BGCM. They also agreed to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents; b. Municipal Accountant and the concerned Disbursing Officers to strictly comply with the proper and timely liquidation of cash advances; c. Concerned municipal officials to strictly comply with the proper and timely liquidation of the fund transfer relative to the SAP-ESP from the DSWD; and d. Concerned municipal officials to provide justifications on the deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the deficiency and promised to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement the GPB and ensure that gender-responsive PPAs were included therein, which are aligned with the measures to mitigate the effects of COVID-19 and the new normal to promote effective and survivor-centered response to all forms of gender-based violence as prescribed by the CHR-DBM JMC 2020-01.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the deficiencies and agreed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to lead the Municipality in its efforts to conduct immediate implementation of COVID-19 response related PPAs to mitigate the risks caused by the COVID 19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials concerned to reconcile their records and reports as to the utilization of the 20% DF and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification. Also, ensure that the quarterly reports on the utilization of the 20% DF are submitted to DOF-BLGF as prescribed by the JMC.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the LDRRMO to strictly follow the guidelines in the preparation of LDRRMFIP and to submit to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), the Department of the Interior and Local Government-Municipal Local Government Operations Office (DILG-MLGOO) and the COA, a copy of the fully compliant LDRRMFIP; b. require the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF is submitted within the prescribed period: and c. direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly abide with the prescribed accounting policy for the proper recognition of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

For the year 2020, the Municipality appropriated a total amount of P49,254,688.00 out of the total appropriations of P954,897,987.00. During the said year, a total amount of P48,819,395.00 was reported to be utilized for various GAD related programs, projects and activities.
Per the records of the Accounting and Treasury Officers, the Municipality was not a recipient of ODA fund.

For CY 2020, the Municipality had withheld P34,206,205.59 (excluding the beginning balance of P5,579,150.69) from its various payments and remitted P33,780,598.31 to the BIR, representing amounts withheld from the salaries and other emoluments/income of its employees and the required taxes from the claims of various suppliers/ contractors/other claimants.
Details of Notice of Suspensions, Notice of Disallowances and Notice of Charges are shown in Annex H.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to immediately submit (a) report on the result of its examination/validation of the Contractor's claims on its request of contract time extension/suspension; (b) the written consent of extension of the validity period of the Contractor's security from the insurance company; and (c) the corresponding letter of approval to all request in order to support the propriety/ validity of the revision of the start and completion dates of the project. Also that the OIC-Municipal Engineer to submit the complete descriptions and cost segregation of road components and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records with the General Services Officer and make necessary adjustments in order to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the non implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
