observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to correctly
classify the outstanding balance of long-term loans payable by presenting the
portion of loans due for settlement within 12 months after the reporting date as part
of the current liabilities and the remaining balance which is due beyond one year
after the reporting date as non-current liabilities in the Statement of Financial
Position.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Accountant and City General Services Officer to:
a. investigate/determine the causes of discrepancies in prior years between their
respective records and make necessary adjustments to ensure the accuracy,
reliability and existence of PPE account balances as reflected in the financial
statements; and
b. undertake the immediate reconciliation of PPEs recorded in different amounts
per books and per RPCPPE with a net difference of P1,033,458.00 and
accordingly make the necessary adjustments.
City Accountant to:
a. book up unrecorded 670 PPE items with the accumulated cost of P81,361,098.02
and the unrecognized depreciation;
b. record in the books donated 40 units e-trikes with a total cost of P21,328,988.92
and its related accumulated depreciation once the copy of Property Transfer
Report is received; and
c. update recording of PPE items recorded as lump-sum of P6,900,229.00 and
PPEs already delivered but not yet paid of P22,568,844.52 or a total of
P29,469,073.52 by recording them separately by item/kind/category with a
complete description, property number and location for easy identification/
comparison of each PPE item with the property records which is necessary for
the reconciliation of PPE accounts.
City General Services Officer to:
a. include in the RPCPPE acquired eight parcels of land and one sports equipment
totaling P58,458,735.00;
b. include in the RPCPPE donated 40 e-trikes units with a total amount of
P21,328,988.92;
c. exclude in the RPCPPE semi-expendable property totaling P12,501,521.97 and
ensure that all these were covered by Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) upon
issuance for monitoring and control purposes;
d. reclassify the PPE items amounting to P5,681,177.00 in the RPCPPE in the same
way as the accounting records by type as prescribed under Section 57 and
Annex 49 of the MNGAS for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume II to
facilitate reconciliation; and
e. include in the recording of PPE incidental costs directly attributable to bringing
the asset to the location and condition necessary for it to be capable of
operating in the manner intended by the City.
OIC of the e-trike Program to:
a. furnish the City Accounting Office and City General Services Office with copies
of Property Transfer Report of the donated 40 e-trikes units to be able to record
the same both in the books and the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. draw adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous recording of advance
payment of RPT and SET in CY 2019 by:
(cid:120) debiting Prior Period Adjustment and Discount on RPT accounts by
P13,136,642.80 and P2,189,486.03, respectively and crediting RPT account
by P15,326,128.83 in the General Fund; and
(cid:120) debiting Prior Period Adjustment and Discount on SET accounts by
P19,704,983.74 and P2,189,486.03, respectively and crediting SET account by
P21,894,469.77 in the Special Education Fund.
b. strictly observe the accrual method of accounting and the provisions pertaining
to the collection of advance payments of RPT and SET.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and/or the City
Treasurer to:
a. send notice to the concerned payees of the four unclaimed checks and submit
thereafter the related DVs upon release of the checks;
b. exert extra efforts to locate the 17 missing DVs and submit the same to the Office
of the Auditor to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension which may mature into
disallowance;
c. produce the documents needed to support the five DVs with lacking documents
and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor to avoid issuance of Notice of
Suspension which may mature into disallowance if not complied within ninety
days from the date of receipt thereof; and
d. observe timeliness in the submission of paid DVs and its supporting documents
to the Office of the Auditor within ten days after the end of the month in
compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Treasurer to prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly and
forward the same to the City Accountant; and
b. City Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) at year-end to
record the restoration of unreleased checks and recognize the
corresponding liability account to reflect the correct balance of the affected
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. assess the collectability of dormant receivable accounts and exert extra effort
in the collection of the same;
b. set-up the appropriate allowance for impairment for possible losses; and
c. if these receivables have been proven to be uncollectible and worthless,
derecognize the assets and the corresponding allowance for impairment from
the books of accounts through write-off of dormant account guided by the
provisions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 on Guidelines and
Procedures on the Write-Off of Dormant Receivable Accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to enforce the
liquidation of CAs strictly within the prescribed period and ensure that all CAs
are liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We also recommended that all personnel in the National Government Agencies
(NGAs), particularly those assigned in the City School Division Office of the
Department of Education (DepEd), be required to secure a clearance from
money and property accountabilities before resignation/retirement/transfer to
another office/agency.

We reiterated our previous (cid:92)ear(cid:182)s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the
City Accountant to strictly require the accountable officers to liquidate their CAs
supported with complete documentary requirements within the prescribed
period.

We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and/or
Heads of Department/Office to evaluate/study first the need to designate
additional SDOs/DOs for the special purpose and time-bound undertakings.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and City General Services
Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects;
b. City Accountant to provide proper disclosure of the Road Network Systems
in the Notes to Financial Statements and maintain Local Road Network
Ledger Cards; and
c. City General Services Officer to maintain Local Road Inventory, Road Map
and Local Road Network Property Cards.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. revert the five A/Ps without valid claims totaling P100,573.06 to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No.
1445;
b. coordinate with the concerned payees of the two A/Ps totaling P26,848,078.00
for the completion of the required lacking documents to proceed with the
settlement of obligations; and
c. ensure that all past due and valid unpaid obligations are covered by perfected
contract duly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. draw the necessary adjustments to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal
accounts; and
b. ensure the correctness of JEVs being drawn before posting the same to
General Ledger to avoid errors in the recording of transactions between
reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and the Provincial Treasurer
to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs
accounts of the City as against their respective reciprocal accounts in the books of
the Province and effect the necessary adjustments accordingly in the books.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to analyze current
and prior years transactions in all funds affecting the Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported
balances of the said accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended
that the City Accountant be required to conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal
accounts to eliminate the difference.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Assessor and City
Treasurer to:
a. ensure that the recorded real properties under the then Municipality of Biñan
and National Housing Authority were registered under those agencies:
(cid:120) if so, exclude those real properties from the list of delinquent taxpayers
and in the yearly assessment; or
(cid:120) if those real properties were proven to be already transferred to a private
individual, update the list of delinquent taxpayers with the name of the
registered owner of the properties and enforce collection of delinquent
RPT.
b. verify the registered owners of the real properties recorded under the name
of the homeowner(cid:182)s association. See to it that they are duly registered with
the appropriate government agency to acquire legal personality, thereby
allowing the City to enforce collection of delinquent RPT.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CSWDO to expedite the
implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Program in accordance with the
covering Specific Implementation Agreement, in order for the beneficiaries to enjoy
the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. submit the lacking Inspection and Acceptance Reports immediately;
b. fill up the POs with all the necessary information pertaining to the purchase
to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the
terms and conditions of the contract; and
c. ensure that all claims are supported with complete documentation to comply
with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC of the E-Trike Program to:
a. enter into MOA with every e-trike driver/operator to establish responsibilities of
both parties and terms and conditions with regards to the transfer of ownership
to the drivers/operators; and
b. strictly implement the terms and conditions of the MOA, including the collection
of leasing fee (boundary) on a daily basis as gradual payment for the e-trikes,
including its proper remittance to the Cit(cid:92) Treasurer(cid:182)s Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to provide
sufficient disclosure of the LDDRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Budget Officer to immediately furnish the GAD Office with SAAOB for CY
2019 which is their basis of GAD Accomplishments of other offices of the City;
b. GAD Officer to finish the preparation and submit the GAD Accomplishment
Report for CY 2019 immediately to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. GAD Officer to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 on the
timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment
Report to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from the receipt of
the approved plan from the DILG and five working days from the end of January
of the preceding year, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the entire
Municipalitys PPE and to conduct reconciliation of the results of the
inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancy
between the inventory report and the accounting and property records
should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error
was committed. Submit to the Auditor the reconciled RPCPPE, pursuant to
Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

b. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to ensure that PPELCs and PCs are
being maintained, respectively. Both records should always be updated and
reconciled;

c. MGSO to update the Property Acknowledgment Receipts of all properties of
the Municipality and ensure compliance on the regulations regarding the
issuance, maintenance and renewal of the document; and

d. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete lapsing schedule for all the
depreciable properties of the Municipality and furnish the Audit Team for
verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to continuously conduct a thorough review and
reconciliation of the reported transactions of the Cash - Local Treasury and CIB
- LCCA accounts in the accounting ledgers and Treasurys cashbooks in order
to determine the causes of the discrepancies and immediately effect the
necessary adjustments to avoid the accumulation of the discrepancy, as well as
to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the
Municipalitys financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to come
up with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of the
taxpayers and the amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a reliable
RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances; and

b. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the
rules on the setting-up of the RPT and SET Receivables to ensure the fair
presentation of the Municipalitys financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC-Municipal Engineer to submit the required report on the complete
description and cost segregation of road components to the GSO and
Accounting Office;

b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule for Road Networks
and make the necessary adjustments to correct the records and balances of
the Road Network Systems and its related Accumulated Depreciation and
Depreciation Expense accounts;

c. GSO to maintain and/or prepare Local Road Inventory and Road Map,
LRNPC and RLRN in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

d. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
total road network system of the Municipality as required in COA Circular
No. 2015-008.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to exhaust all possible means to collect these
receivables; otherwise, review and refile the request for write-off to the Audit
Team Leader/ Supervising Auditor provided that the basis for denial has been
satisfactorily complied with/met.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and
Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the
affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipalitys financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to review/analyze all the existing fund transfers in the books
and retrieve the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) and/or Trust
Agreements relative therewith to properly utilize the available funds; and

b. Implementing Office/Division and/or personnel assigned to fast track the
implementation and accomplishment of the projects so that the benefits
would be delivered to the intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible
time. Otherwise, the unutilized funds must be returned to the source NGAs
in order that it may be made available for other priority programs or projects
of said government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the utilization of the funds
from shares of PCSO Lotto draws and STL draws in accordance with the
purposes cited under Republic Act No. 1169 for the benefit of its indigent
constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the Chart of Accounts in the
recording of the Municipalitys share from PCSO Charity Fund for better control
purposes; and

b. ensure that income from all sources are properly appropriated, allotted and
obligated only for duly approved programs, projects and activities during the
budget year.

We reiterated our previous year's recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
 Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all property; (b) prepare and submit the required inventory report thereon to the Audit Team.
 Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to ensure that PPELCs and PCs are being maintained, respectively. Both records should always be updated and reconciled;
 MGSO to update the Property Acknowledgment Receipts of all properties of the Municipality and ensure compliance on the regulations regarding the issuance, maintenance and renewal of the document; and
 Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete lapsing schedule for all the depreciable properties of the Municipality and furnish the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: Conducted a complete and fixed assets inventory of the Municipality. The Municipal Accounting Office effected the necessary correcting entries thru JEV Nos. 100-19-06-4224D dated June 21, 2019. Continued to maintain records but still were not complete. Lapsing Schedule is still incomplete and without details as to the computation of the provision for Depreciation.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to further intensify the reconciliation of their respective records to determine the reasons for the discrepancies and immediately effect the necessary corrections in the General Ledger in order to ensure the completeness of the recorded transactions as well as the accuracy of the balance of Cash in Bank account; and
 Municipal Accountant to immediately investigate the reconciling items discovered during the reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments/recording of the reconciling items presented in the BRS for the correct presentation as at the end of the year in the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Conducted regular reconciliation of records of Cash in Bank and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the reasons for the discrepancies and immediately effect the necessary adjustments to avoid the accumulation of the discrepancy, as well as to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements. The identified reconciling items were significantly been reduced from prior year’s amount of P32,698,187.71 to P1,867,725.24 as at year-end.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exhaust all possible means to collect these receivables; otherwise, review and refile the request for write-off to the Audit Team Leader/ Supervising Auditor provided that the basis for denial has been satisfactorily complied with/met.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the Audit Team’s audit observation.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately provide the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables in order to present their correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary entries in the Municipality's books to present the correct balances of Due from LGUs, Due to LGUs and RPT Income accounts; and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate/request with the concerned Provincial Official, for the details of the property tax, that the property tax had already been made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant effected the necessary correcting journal entries. The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Provincial Official for the details of the property tax.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMB to:
 Compel the Barangays who have no MRFs to follow all provisions of RA No. 9003 particularly the establishment of MRF and segregation of waste at source in order to reduce the volume of waste being disposed to the final facility; and
 ensure close supervision with monitoring of the Barangays’ compliance and monitor the establishment of fully functional MRF in all its Barangays.
observations.managementAction: Monitored the Barangays’ compliance. MRF activity in some Barangays was limited to the sorting of garbage. Continued monitoring of the Barangays’ MRF activity in some Barangays was conducted.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and the Heads of the Implementing Units to observe/maintain coordination with each other for the effective implementation of GAD PPAs and for the benefit of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to utilize funds for GAD related PPAs for CY 2019 amounting to P53,072,384.41 for GAD PPAs.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and other concerned Municipal Officials to immediately replenish the Trust Liability-DRRM account in order to present its correct balance and to have sufficient fund for the Municipality's disaster risk reduction and management programs/activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office effected the necessary correcting entries thru JEV No. 100-19-06-4224A dated 06/21/2019.

observations.reason: There are cars covered by conduction sticker only.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant
and City General Services Officer to:
a. Evaluate the discrepancies between their records and make the necessary
adjustments; and
b. Update PCs and PPELCs for each category of asset, respectively, and see to it
that reconciliation of their records is finished.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City General Services Officer to exclude in the inventory report the cost of
Computer Software Licenses;
b. City Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop the small
tangile assets from the PPE accounts; and
c. City General Services Officer to ensure that all small tangible assets are
covered by ICS.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. Facilitate the immediate submission of the 66 DVs and their supporting
documents; and
b. Strictly adhere to the submission of accounts within the period prescribed
under Section 100 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006. Since this
unjustified inability to submit said accounts is a repeated violation and
disregard for laws and regulations, the remedies provided under Section 122
of PD No. 1445 could be instituted under the circumstances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct a
thorough review and analysis of all outstanding accounts payable and if found to
be valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the
payment/settlement of the accounts; otherwise, revert back to unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund in accordance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to recognize in
the books accrued interest of P16,299,484.57 by debiting the account “Prior
Period Adjustment” and crediting “Interest Payable” both by the same amount, in
compliance with the accrual method of accounting.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Market Committee to review and evaluate the market operations,
specifically the collection of rental fees and strictly implement the policy on
the cancellation of contract of lease of defaulting stallholders; and
b. City Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the uncollected rental
fees that are deemed realizable by debiting the account “Operating Lease
Receivable” and crediting “Receipts from Market Operation” for current
year transactions and “Prior Period Adjustment” for prior year’s
transactions.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Market Committee to:
a. Cancel/Terminate the Contract of Lease of stallholders listed in Annex J
who have subleased their stalls to another person;
b. Send letters to the sub-leased tenants to vacate the stalls they have
occupied immediately; and
c. Declare the sub-leased stalls vacant and henceforth advertise/post in a
conspicuous place open to all interested lessees/tenants.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct
the City Accountant to require the immediate liquidation by concerned
accountable officers of their outstanding cash advances totaling P540,321.42
in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and henceforth see to it that all
outstanding cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct
the City Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any
official, officer or employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance,
and to designate a Special Disbursing Officer for the grant of additional cash
advances for special activities/projects.

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to make
representation with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) to fully adopt the
ATM for employees of the City that are still not yet covered by existing ATM
payroll system to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to DO.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. Strictly require the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances
supported with documentary requirements within the prescribed period;
and
b. Avail of the remedies as provided by COA Circular No. 97-002 and CSC
Resolution No. 12-00103 for non-compliance with the pertinent provisions
of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct
the City Treasurer to increase the bonding requirements of the accountable
officers, pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 to
protect the interest of the City Government, otherwise ensure that cash
advance granted is within the limit of the fidelity bond.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Budget Officer to allocate in the Annual Budget an amount for the titling of
acquired real property of the City;
b. City General Services Officer, City Assessor and City Treasurer to exert extra
effort in locating the pertinent financial and legal documents of the
undocumented lot/s amounting to P12,711,401.66 to protect the interest of the
City; and
c. City General Services Officer, City Assessor and City Treasurer to prioritize the
registration and transfer of title of the 69 parcels of land in the name of the City
of Biñan in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445, Article 47(c) of IRR
of RA No. 7160 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor advise the
City Accountant to analyze and evaluate further the collectability of the long
outstanding receivables of P3,279,885.34. If these receivables have been found to
be uncollectible and worthless, derecognize the asset account through write-off of
dormant accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 16, 2016. Also, set up appropriate Allowance for Impairment for the
possible losses.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the City Mayor:
a. Direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake an
appraisal of these unserviceable property and prepare a corresponding report
thereon considering the list of unserviceable property and other required
documents were forwarded to them last June 13, 2018; and
b. Once these appraisals and the documents required in the disposal are
complete, direct the City General Services Officer to submit to the Office of the
Auditor a request for disposal of all unserviceable property together with the
IIRUP, Appraisal Report and other required documents for his/her appropriate
action.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to:
a. Cause the transfer of registration of the motor vehicles in the name of the City
and follow-up with the Land Transportation Office (LTO) the immediate release of
the plate numbers; and keep a file copy of Car Registrations (CRs);
b. See to it that issued AREs are completely filled-up and duly signed by the
concerned accountable officers; and
c. Issue AREs to authorized accountable officers or department heads instead to
Job Order personnel.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the City Mayor:
a. Ensure that all insurable property are covered by property insurance with the
General Insurance Fund of the GSIS pursuant to RA No. 656, as amended and
COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992; and
b. Direct the City General Services Officer to facilitate the preparation and
submission of the list of property not yet insured so that the necessary
insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the
General Insurance Fund.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City
Engineer to observe the publicity requirements for government programs,
projects and activities as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004. Likewise,
see to it that the cost of public notices is included in the computation of the
program of work in all of the infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Submit copies of the MOA, Trust Agreement or MOU with the source agency
detailing the terms of reference in the implementation of the project to avoid
the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance;
b. Direct the City Accountant to submit the list of beneficiaries together with their
acceptance to support the disbursements for the livelihood programs listed in
Annex R; and
c. Henceforth, ensure that BuB projects are covered with MOA, Trust Agreement or
MOU by and between the City of Biñan and the source agency, detailing the
provisions for fund utilization and disposition of unexpended portion.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the Chief of
Hospital to adhere strictly to the guidelines prescribed in the PhilHealth Circular
No. 011-2011 on the implementation of the NBB Policy to support the most
vulnerable groups including the indigent people of their medical care needs.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Chief of Hospital to ensure
that sufficient medicines, medical and laboratory supplies are available within the
hospital to refrain from reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses incurred by the
indigent patients.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to:
a. Require the contractor to rectify within 15 days upon receipt of the notice the
deficiencies noted in the COA TAS inspection report;
b. Submit Final/Revised Accomplishment Report; and
c. In case of inability of the contractor to rectify the deficiencies within the
prescribed period, demand the payment for the equivalent cost deficiency of
P5,076,426.12.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to submit to the
Office of the Auditor the above-mentioned documents to facilitate technical review
and inspection by COA-TAS: (a) Quantity Cost Analysis and Detailed Site Plan as
required under item Nos. 2(g) and 3(c) of Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184; (b) Pictures before, during and after construction and As-Built Plans
pursuant to Item Nos. 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and
(c) complete set of approved detailed plans for Phases 1 and 2 of the projects and
Certificate of Project Completion as required in Item 4 of Annex A of the 2016
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and as requested in the Inspection Report for final
validation of the project.

We recommended that the City Mayor reconstitute the CWMB membership by
filling-up the position of the Chairperson of the Sangguniang Kabataan (SK)
Federation to conform with the membership requirements of Section 5, Rule VI of
the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:
a. Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for
the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team; and
b. Comply with the submission of the GPB and AR to the Provincial Planning and
Development Office and the DILG Provincial Office for their review and
endorsement as prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01
dated January 12, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical inventory of
all the Municipality’s fixed assets and prepare and submit the required report
thereon. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting
and property records should be investigated and make the necessary
adjustments where the error was committed;
b. Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete PPE ledger cards for all PPE
accounts; reconcile the discrepancies between the balances in the PPE
ledgers and general ledger;
c. Municipal General Services Officer to maintain a complete and updated
property cards, as a counter check to the correctness of the accounting
records; and
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete Lapsing Schedule to support
the PPE balances in the Financial Statements as well as the provision made
for Depreciation Expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a regular
reconciliation of their respective records to ensure completeness of the
recorded transactions as well as the accuracy of the balance of Cash in Bank
account;
b. Municipal Treasurer to assign personnel that would specifically undertake
the validation/verification of the monthly cash analysis, prepared by the
Accounting Office, in order to facilitate reconciliation; and
c. Municipal Accountant, whenever book reconciling item occurs in the
preparation of BRS, to immediately investigate the same and secure the
corresponding document as basis for recording in the book; and to strictly
monitor the outstanding checks for possible maturing of the same into stale
check for immediate adjustment, to avoid accumulation of book reconciling
items, such as outstanding checks and stale checks, for correct presentation
of the balance of Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a. Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal General Services
Officer and the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and
detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects of
the Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and
proper recognition the same in the books of accounts; and
b. Municipal General Services Office to conduct a complete and accurate
inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality as required
under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor, submit to the Municipal
Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing their names and the amount
collectible from them at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real
Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables, in accordance with Section 20
of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

Also, the Municipal Accountant, with the help of the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Information System (MIS), come up with the details of the accounts
showing the complete list of the taxpayers and the amounts collectibles from
them in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a. Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) to assess/re-examine, in
collaboration with the DSWD, its project proposal for the immediate
approval of the same. Also, to avoid delay due to change of personnel acting
as the BuB Focal Person, involve the former BuB Focal Person in the
implementation of BUB project started during his/her time until completely
finished; and
b. BuB Focal Person to optimally utilize the funds for the intended purposes
and to abide strictly to what were stipulated in the respective Memorandum
of Agreement (MOA), most specially on the liquidation of funds with the
concerned source agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a. Municipal Officials concerned to furnish the Audit Team the copy of the
approved contract together with pertinent documents, which are deemed
necessary in the conduct of auditorial review, and in the technical evaluation
thereof; and
b. BAC Secretariat to comply with the required posting of information in the
Municipality’s website to uphold transparency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Local
School Board to provide allocations for the Early Childhood Care and Development
(ECCD) programs, projects and activities in the Special Education Fund (SEF)
Annual Budget in addition to the other local funds in accordance to Section 7 (b) of
Republic Act No. 10410, otherwise known as the Early Years Act of 2013 for the
benefit of parents and children beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to provide budgetary line item in the annual budget at least one
percent of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment exclusively for the
strengthening and implementation of Programs, Projects and Activities of the Local
Council for the Protection of Children.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission
of all their accounts and financial reports, in adherence to the provisions of Section
7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006 and Section 6.03, 6.04 and 6.05 of COA Circular
No. 95-006, to allow the Audit Team to conduct timely review/post audit of their
accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to demand immediate settlement of all unliquidated cash advances from officials and employees concerned pursuant to above cited regulations. If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who persistently fail or refuse to comply despite repeated demand for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them to compel liquidation. Further, ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advance, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Henceforth, ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced.

The said PFPR Plan should be integrated in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to demand immediate settlement of all unliquidated cash advances from officials and employees concerned pursuant to above cited regulations. If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who persistently fail or refuse to comply despite repeated demand for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them to compel liquidation.  Further, ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advance, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Henceforth, ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced.  Monitor the outstanding cash advances of officials and employees and demand immediate settlement after its purpose has been served.
